description:
You are helping a neuroscience PhD student who keeps her experimental
    recordings under

        /home/user/datasets/raw/

    and wants an automatic, date–stamped, **compressed** backup created every
    night at 02:30.  The compressed file must be written to

        /home/user/datasets/backups/raw_daily_YYYYMMDD.tar.gz

    where the literal string YYYYMMDD is produced at run-time with the
    familiar `date +%Y%m%d` format string.

    1. Add a cron entry to the *current* user’s crontab that:
       • runs at 02 : 30 every day  
       • executes a single `tar -czf …` command that
         a) creates the archive named exactly  
            /home/user/datasets/backups/raw_daily_$(date +\%Y\%m\%d).tar.gz  
         b) archives the entire directory  
            /home/user/datasets/raw/  
       • appends both standard output and standard error to a running
         logfile located at  
            /home/user/backup.log

    2. After installing the job, **export the exact contents of the newly
       installed crontab** to the file

        /home/user/cron_verify.log

       The file must contain **only one line** (plus its terminating linefeed):
       the cron specification itself—no headers, no blank lines, no comments.
       Automated grading will compare that file byte-for-byte, so keep the
       spacing exactly as shown below.

    Exact required line (note the leading spaces between the five time fields):

        30 2 * * * tar -czf /home/user/datasets/backups/raw_daily_$(date +\%Y\%m\%d).tar.gz /home/user/datasets/raw >> /home/user/backup.log 2>&1

    3. Do everything from the normal non-privileged shell; no `sudo`
       may be used.

    Summary of artefacts the grader will look for:

       • Presence of the above cron line in the user’s crontab  
       • File /home/user/cron_verify.log containing **exactly** that one line  
         followed by a single trailing newline

    A single, correctly-formed terminal command line is enough to carry
    out both steps (cron installation and export), but feel free to reach
    the goal any way you like as long as the final state matches the
    specification above.
truth:
Pre-existing filesystem objects before the agent starts:

       /home/user/datasets/                  (directory, 755)
       /home/user/datasets/raw/              (directory, 755, may be empty)
       /home/user/datasets/backups/          (directory, 755, may be empty)

    Desired final state after successful completion:

    1. The user’s crontab (queried with `crontab -l`) must consist solely of:

       30 2 * * * tar -czf /home/user/datasets/backups/raw_daily_$(date +\%Y\%m\%d).tar.gz /home/user/datasets/raw >> /home/user/backup.log 2>&1

       plus its trailing newline, and *nothing else*.

    2. The file /home/user/cron_verify.log must exist, be world-readable
       (644) and contain exactly the single line above followed by one
       newline character (0x0A).  File size: 132 bytes.

    3. No other new files should be created anywhere except the log file
       mentioned above and whatever default files the `crontab`
       implementation itself uses internally (which reside under
       /var/spool/cron/ and are out of scope for the agent).
